Deceased parents and sister Thelma I Beighley.

aged 88yrs 6das. nee Libitzer. of Highbury Rd, Harmar, died Saturday. Born in Harmar she lived there all her life. Member of Pine Creek Presbyterian Church, Indiana Twp. Survived by sons Willliam Myers of Harmar and Allen Myers of Indiana Twp; dau Mrs Felix (Joanne) Rocco of Saltsburg, 8 gch, 4 ggch. Deceased husband Emanual Myers, parents Frank and Martha Weigand Libitzer, 4 brothers and a sister. Funeral Aug 4 1992, Rev Diana Harbinson.

aged 19. Killed in the Kinloch mine explosion

aged 78. son of David and Orie (Laforte). of 40 Zane Ave, Kinloch, Lower Burrell died in the afternoon. Injured in a fire in his home on Apr 4. Born in Castle Shannon, he was in this community since 1918. Employed at Alcoa for 40 years, he retired in 1966. He was a Protestant. Survivors: brother John of New Kensington, sisters, Mrs Elizabeth Zeiler of New Kensington, Mrs Minnie Korzon of Lower Burrell and Mrs Ida Mae Nold of Spring City. Service conducted by Rev Mark H Landfried pastor of the United Pres. Church, New Kensington.

aged 84. nee Shrader. Obit: of Lower Burrell, in Citizens General Hospital, New Kensington. Born in Monessen, came to the area in 1931. Member of the United Pres. Church, New Kensington. Survived by son James L Oliver of Lower Burrell, 3 gch, sister June Hopkins of Charleroi. Deceased, husband John Oliver in 1983, parents, five brothers Don, Claude, Cliff, George and Duane Schrader, sister Betty. Officiating services Rev L Kenneth Hooten II.
aged 61. son of David and Orie.

aged 75. nee Myers, dau of Philip & Jennie (Brooks). Wife of William John. Obit: of Upper Burrell Twp, died at 10 am. Born in New Kensington and lived there all her life. Survived by dau Mrs Anthony (Jennie) Knosky, who lived with her; sons David of Framinghan MA, Phillip of Oakmont, and Harry of New Kensington, sister Mrs Joseph (Evelyn) Glaister of Upper Burrell, brother Edward Myers of Detroit, 15 gch, 18 ggch. Her husband William John Oliver was killed in the Kinloch mine explosion in 1929. Services at Ross G Walker Funeral Home, conducted by Rev J Harry Fisher, pastor of the Puckety United Pres. Church of Lower Burrell.

aged 69. son of David & Orie (Laforte). Ambulance driver WWII Vet. of 355 Freeport st, New Kensington died Saturday at 3:15pm. Born in Castle Shannon he lived most of his life in New Kensington. A crane operator at Alcoa with 39 yrs service, retiring in 1969. A WWII vet serving as an ambulance driver from July 1943 to March 1946 with the 239th General Hospital. Protestant. Survivors: wife of 46 years, Eleanor Shrader Oliver, son James L of Culloden WV, grandson, sister Minnie Korzon of Lower Burrell, Elizabeth Zeiler of New Kensington, and Ida Mae Nold of Spring City. Deceased, brothers David Thomas and George. Services by Rev Mark Landfried.

son of Stephen & Mary (Melo). Wife of Mary Lou. Cheswick mayor & Fire Chief. aged 72. AMM3 USN WWII Vet. died at home. Born in Tarentum, graduate of Fellsmere High Sch, Fellsmere, FL. WWII vet. Millwright at Edgewater Steel, Oakmont before retiring. Former mayor of Cheswick, president of borough council, fire chief. St Francis of Assisi RC Church in Harmar. Survived by wife of 47 yrs, Mary Lou Simon Sekeras, dau Mary Ann Bell, Mrs Eric (Sue) Strawoet, both of Cheswick, and Mrs Emil (Patty Jane) Scarano of Murrysville, 5 gch, sister Sophie Delenne of Cheswick. Deceased parents, Stephen and Mary (Melo), brother Stephen, sister Anne Gramling. Burket-Truby Funeral Home conducted by Rev Hugh McCormley.

aged 80. nee Johnson dau of Anton and Hilda (Johnson). Wife of William H. Obit: of Leechburg Rd, Lower Burrell, born in Natrona, Harrison, she lived in the valley all her life. 1935 graduate of New Kensington High Sch., member Braeburn United Methodist Ch. In the choir, Sec. of PTA, and much else. Survived by husband of 52 yrs Wm H Tingle, son Wm HC Tingle IV of Hanover NH, dau Mrs Marshall (Susan) Krumpe of Hanover, 5 gch, sister Jean McLure of Lower Burrell. Deceased parents, brother JW 'Scotty' Johnson, sister Lula Van Tine. Service by Rev Ralph W Brownfield.

nee McRoberts dau aged 90, of Charles Craik & Elizabeth M (Wachter). Wife of William C, Jr. Obit: N Margaret Tingle. Of Leechburg Rd, Lower Burrell, born in Braddock, and lived most of her life in the Alle-Kiske valley. Harmarville United Presbyterian Church. She and her husband operated a grocery store for years in the Lower Burrell area, and cooked and sold 'Tingle's BBQ' from their home. Lived in Rogue River OR for 15 yrs. Survived by son William H Tingle of Lower Burrell, sister Opal G McRoberts of Oakmont Presbyterian Home, formerly of Harmar Twp, 2 gch, 4 ggch. Deceased: husband Wm C Tingle in 1972, brother Wm C McRoberts, sisters edna Harrison and mae McRoberts. Services by Rev Frank E Magor

aged 65. formerly of New Kensington, died in the home of his daughter Mrs Albert (Dorothy) Siciliano, 1414 Milan St. Brookline. Born in McDonald, lived most of his life in New Kensington. Three yrs ago moved to Brookline. Member First Presbyterian Ch, McKeesport. Watchman for OK Cleaners, McKeesport. Survived by widow, Wlizabeth Oliver Zeiler, sons William, McKeesport, Elmer, Henry, Raymond, New Kensington, Lloyd of Pittsburgh, daughters Mrs Siciliano, Mrs James (Helen) Kovach and Mrs Leo (Ida Mae) Salzinoff of New Kensington.
